operator_directive_processed.deliverable_required: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-audit-files-round-trip.json with diagnostic findings + concrete fix patches + counter-arguments

tasks_summary.task_1_confirm_mismatch: Confirmed. GitHub Tree API vs FUSE recursive listing shows: monitor/integrity has 172 git-only files (narrative-cite-audit + verify-pending-run + workspace-sync-runs combined). All other monitor/ subdirs are balanced or have small FUSE-only excesses (different bug class — pending workspace-sync push). The cutoff on FUSE is precisely 2026-05-17T14:20Z (consistent FUSE mtime across all three affected categories).
tasks_summary.task_2_trace_round_trip: Identified. Writers (audit-narrative-citations.js, verify-pending-state.js, workspace-sync inline run-report block) all write cwd-relative inside workspace-sync's clone. Files get pushed to git via workspace-sync's commit. Clone is rm-rf'd in Step 5. The reverse direction (git → FUSE) is the build.js syncToWorkspace() walker (PROP-010), invoked by decider after self-apply (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md line 508) or by operator-direct build.js publish. Workspace-sync.md explicitly never invokes it. When decider's publish cadence drops (e.g., revision-routed work, low patch volume), the round-trip starves.
tasks_summary.task_3_choose_fix: Chose Option A (make round-trip work). Single-line addition at end of workspace-sync.md Step 4b invokes existing PROP-010 syncToWorkspace() walker via `node build.js sync-workspace`. Rejected Option B (clone-only files) because three writers would need modification, race conditions with workspace-sync's existing Step 2, and existing PROP-010 plumbing already correctly handles the case.
tasks_summary.task_4_counter_arguments: Addressed (a) intentional clone-only: refuted by state-verification.md line 139 + historical FUSE state. (b) Stale FUSE ls: refuted by fresh find + 3 independent categories showing same cutoff. (c) Re-create 720MB bloat: YES if applied before PROP-051 Workstream C pruning, NO if sequenced correctly. (d) Other unknown categories: ran bucket-level diagnostic showing only monitor/integrity/ has the git-only-extra pattern; everything else either matches or has FUSE-only-extra (inverse direction). Plus two additional counter-arguments added during diagnostic: (e) sync-workspace from wrong clone, (f) pruning race conditions.
tasks_summary.task_5_sequencing: Documented. PROP-052 patches sit in regions of workspace-sync.md that PROP-051 does not touch (P1 in Step 4b at line ~921, P2 in Rules at line ~932). No textual conflicts. Operational sequencing: PROP-051 Workstream A first (safety), then PROP-051 Workstream C (pruning), then PROP-052 (round-trip). If PROP-052 lands before pruning, FUSE balloons by 36MB at current git size, much more at historical sizes.

findings.title: Git→FUSE round-trip starvation for clone-resident audit artifacts

evidence.fuse_vs_git_diff: monitor/integrity git-only: 172 files (~36MB) not on FUSE. Affected categories: narrative-cite-audit (20 git-only at 1.7MB each), workspace-sync-runs (92 git-only), verify-pending-run (85 git-only).
evidence.fuse_cutoff: All three categories stop precisely at 2026-05-17T14:20Z mtime. Git extends to 2026-05-20T22:05Z (the last workspace-sync push before today's disaster).
evidence.last_publish_commit: 2026-05-17T20:22:08Z 'Update review (auto-build 2026-05-17)'. Next was 2026-05-20T06:04Z for PROP-050. So ~3 days with zero publish cycles.

findings.root_cause: There is no dedicated git→FUSE refresh mechanism. The existing one (syncToWorkspace() in build.js) is invoked only by `build.js publish` or `build.js sync-workspace`, neither of which is in workspace-sync.md's procedure. The round-trip is a side-effect of decider's post-self-apply cleanup. When decider's cadence drops, the round-trip starves.
findings.fix_proposed: PROP-052 (this run) — add `node build.js sync-workspace || echo WARN` at end of workspace-sync.md Step 4b. Reuses existing PROP-010 plumbing. Idempotent. Append-only-safe. Non-blocking on failure.
findings.blocked_by: PROP-051 Workstream C (monitor/integrity pruning) — must land first to avoid FUSE bloat.

findings.title: open-issues.json continues JSON-corrupt; blocks tinker PROP-030 backlog-trend pre-flight
findings.evidence: Node.js JSON.parse fails at byte 45760 ('Unexpected token ] before ISS-2172' pattern, per tinker-2026-05-21T02-45 and -05-40 prior observations). Tinker's PROP-030 backlog-trend computation cannot proceed cleanly without parseable open-issues.json.
findings.out_of_scope_note: Not addressed in PROP-052 — separate decider-side bug from the FUSE-only degraded mode. Operator may want to file a third directive specifically for this. Tinker reaffirms the prior runs' observation.
findings.recommended_followup: Inspect open-issues.json structure manually, restore from a recent known-good commit (1d256277 pre-disaster was clean per PROP-051's force-reset target), or hand-fix the stray closing bracket. Workspace-sync's PROP-024 json-validity gate would have refused the original write but didn't fire because decider's clone wrote it directly via FUSE-only degraded mode (which itself was a degraded-mode improvisation similar to the disaster's no-checkout clone).
